summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Five Agra Select B2B landing site. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases passed covering health, quote-requests CRUD, commodity validation, email validation, persistence and sort-desc list. Frontend: All 10 sections render, navbar/anchors/CTAs/language switcher/contact form all functional. Form happy path successfully POSTs to /api/quote-requests (201) and shows success state; form clears after submit. RU and RO translations correctly update hero/section copy, and selection persists across reload (localStorage key 'fa_lang'). Top Seller and Ukraine's Pride badges present on corn and sunflower cards respectively.

issues: Empty-form submission relies on HTML5 native required-field validation (since name/email inputs have the `required` attribute). As a result, the custom JS branch `if (!form.name || !form.email || !form.commodity) setStatus('error')` is effectively unreachable for required text fields; the [data-testid=quote-error] element will only ever surface for axios/network failures. Acceptance criterion said empty form should show quote-error. This is currently satisfied by the browser's native popover instead of in-app messaging. Consider removing `required` from inputs (and validating only via JS) if the in-app quote-error state is desired, or keep as-is since browser validation is generally better UX.

action_items: Optional: align empty-form validation behavior — either remove HTML5 `required` from name/email/commodity inputs to surface the in-app quote-error state, or update the acceptance criterion to accept native browser validation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is clean and small (~106 lines). MongoDB _id is correctly excluded from list responses via projection.

POST /api/quote-requests stores created_at as ISO string but list endpoint re-parses to datetime for response_model — works but slightly inconsistent; could store as native datetime and let motor handle it.

CORS allow_origins from env defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True; this combination is disallowed by browsers (will be rejected if credentials needed). Currently fine because the frontend uses no auth/cookies.
Landing.jsx is ~896 lines (>700 threshold). Consider splitting sections (Hero/Stats/Commodities/etc.) into dedicated component files for maintainability.
ContactSection: the unreachable JS validation branch noted above; either trust HTML5 or remove `required` to actually show quote-error.
seed_data_creation: Created several quote-request documents prefixed with TEST_ in MongoDB collection quote_requests via API during testing (Alice + 5 commodities + PW User from frontend submit).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints and frontend flows verified working end-to-end against external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. data-testid coverage is complete and consistent with the request. Language persistence key is `fa_lang` (not `fa.lang`). Only minor observation: HTML5 required attributes block the in-app `quote-error` from ever firing for an empty submit — kept as a design note, not a defect.
